article_title: Ivonescimab Beats a PD-1 Standard in First-Line Squamous Lung Cancer at ASCO, the First China-Originated Drug to Reach the Society's Plenary
summary: Editorial override to APPROVE — both automated errors are confirmed false positives caused by the parenthesised Lancet DOI URL (orphan-regex truncation) and a bot-blocked (403) pointer source. All content verified.

findings.message: Body cites 1 URL(s) not in article.sources (provenance break)
findings.details:
These URLs appear as Markdown links in body_markdown but are missing from article.sources, so they have no committed snapshot under sources/YYYY-MM/<slug>/ and cannot be verified through the provenance chain. Orphan URL(s):
  - https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/article/PIIS0140-6736(26

findings.message: Both automated errors are FALSE POSITIVES — verdict overridden to APPROVE after full manual verification
findings.details: (1) The 'orphan URL' is the truncated string 'https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/article/PIIS0140-6736(26' — the orphan-detection regex stops at the first ')'. The FULL Lancet URL 'https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lancet/article/PIIS0140-6736(26)00966-9/abstract' IS present in article.sources AND in body_markdown, verbatim and identical; there is no provenance break. (2) The 403 is thelancet.com bot-blocking the fetcher; it is cited only as a primary-publication pointer for descriptors (interim analysis, randomised, double-blind, phase 3) that are independently corroborated by the reachable PR Newswire and CancerNetwork snapshots.

content_preview.title: Ivonescimab Beats a PD-1 Standard in First-Line Squamous Lung Cancer at ASCO, the First China-Originated Drug to Reach the Society's Plenary
content_preview.summary: HARMONi-6 showed Akeso's PD-1/VEGF bispecific cut the risk of death by 34% versus tislelizumab plus chemotherapy, and reached the ASCO plenary for the first time for a China-originated cancer drug.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A bispecific antibody developed in China outperformed a PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor head-to-head in the first-line treatment of advanced squamous non-small cell lung cancer, a setting where survival gains have been hard to come by. According to [The ASCO Post](https://ascopost.com/news/june-2026/overall-survival-benefit-with-ivonescimab-plus-chemotherapy-in-advanced-squamous-nsclc/), patients in the phase 3 HARMONi-6 trial who received ivonescimab plus chemotherapy reached a median ov...

recommendations: No action needed — the automated 'orphan URL' error is a false positive from the parenthesised Lancet DOI; the full URL is present in article.sources and body_markdown. Verdict overridden to APPROVE.

editor_notes.content_quality: Exceptionally well-sourced oncology News piece (763 words) that reports the HARMONi-6 plenary data with full statistical detail, separates the head-to-head design from chemotherapy-only comparisons, and is carefully hedged about generalisability.
editor_notes.source_verification: Three of four snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200); the Lancet (source-3) returned 403 and is a primary-publication pointer only. PR Newswire (s0) confirms verbatim the 'first time a China-originated investigational oncology drug … ASCO Plenary Session in the society's 61-year history' line, the 'first-in-class PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody' descriptor, the HR=0.66 / P=0.0017, and the Lu quote 'HARMONi-6 is the first global Phase III study in lung cancer to show statistically significant improvements in both OS and PFS …'. The ASCO Post (s1) confirms the median OS 27.9 vs 23.7 months with 'hazard ratio = 0.66, 95% CI = 0.50-0.87, one-sided P = .0017', the 532-patient stage IIIB/IIIC/IV enrolment, the 1:1 (266/266) randomisation, the 34% death-risk reduction, the control-arm PD-L1 split (~27 vs ~19 months), and the Lu 'Squamous [NSCLC] is associated with worse clinical outcomes … median survival beyond 2 years' quote with his Shanghai Chest Hospital affiliation. CancerNetwork (s2) confirms every remaining specific verbatim: PFS 11.1 vs 6.9 months (HR 0.60, 95% CI 0.46-0.78, P<0.0001), 12-month OS 78.9%/72.2%, 24-month OS 64.7%/48.6%, the dosing (ivonescimab 20 mg/kg q3w, carboplatin AUC 5, paclitaxel 175 mg/m^2, up to 4 cycles, maintenance up to 24 months; tislelizumab 200 mg q3w), the PD-L1 subgroup HRs (0.64 / 0.68 / 0.67 / 0.64), and the safety figures (any-grade haemorrhage 24.8% vs 12.1%, hypertension 14.7% vs 5.7%, grade >=3 irAE 14% both arms). The Lancet-attributed descriptors (interim, randomised, double-blind, phase 3) are all independently corroborated in the reachable PR Newswire and CancerNetwork snapshots.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every number, hazard ratio, confidence interval, dose, and quote traces verbatim to a reachable cited source — an unusually clean record for a statistic-dense oncology piece. No claim rests solely on the bot-blocked Lancet pointer.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and precise; 'What We Don't Know' properly flags the China-only enrolment, the interim nature of the OS analysis, and the absence of a pembrolizumab comparator.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior ivonescimab / HARMONi-6 coverage in the archive.
concerns: Automated verdict was REJECT due to two false positives: (a) the orphan-URL regex truncates the parenthesised Lancet DOI ('PIIS0140-6736(26)00966-9') at the first ')', but the full URL is in article.sources and body_markdown; (b) the Lancet 403 bot-block on a pointer-only source. Both were manually cleared; verdict overridden to APPROVE.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Fully-verified, statistically rigorous oncology News piece; the automated REJECT was a known parenthesised-URL false positive. APPROVE.
